mechanism_narrative: Positive retail sales momentum raises expected near-term corporate revenue growth and earnings revisions; higher 3-month growth in RSAFS predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS latest available value was 447255 as of t-1mo in the decision snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if rolling 3-year walk-forward tests show positive RSAFS momentum followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in no more than 50% of observations, or if mean triggered return is not above the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation accelerates while the federal funds rate stays near zero, investors price tighter future policy and lower equity valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year change crosses above 3% while FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS latest available value was 0.12 as of t-1mo in the decision snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes deliver positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases, or if adding the CPIAUCSL-by-FEDFUNDS interaction does not improve out-of-sample log loss versus single-series signals.

mechanism_narrative: Falling unemployment combined with payroll expansion signals durable income growth and lower recession risk, raising expected earnings and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months and PAYEMS rises over the same period
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE latest available value was 5.0 as of t-1mo in the decision snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if, during triggered labor-recovery episodes, 60-day S&P 500 returns are not higher than unconditional 60-day returns, or if the hit rate for positive returns is below 55% over rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in industrial production signals weakening real activity and operating leverage pressure, causing lower expected earnings and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO latest available value was 107.0917 as of t-2mo in the decision snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if triggered INDPRO contractions are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the conditional mean return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A material decline in housing starts signals weaker household formation, construction activity, and durable-goods demand, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST latest available value was 1206.0 as of t-2mo in the decision snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST contraction triggers do not predict below-median 60-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of triggered observations, or if the rolling Rank IC is negative in less than half of 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the federal funds rate raises discount rates and tightens financial conditions, reducing equity valuation support and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS 3-month change crosses above 0.25 percentage points
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS latest available value was 0.12 as of t-1mo in the decision snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if positive 5-day S&P 500 returns occur in more than 60% of FEDFUNDS tightening triggers, or if the average triggered return is not below the unconditional 5-day average return.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate signals weakening household income, rising credit risk, and recession probability, leading investors to reduce equity exposure and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE 3-month change crosses above 0.3 percentage points
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE latest available value was 5.0 as of t-1mo in the decision snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if triggered unemployment increases are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the conditional drawdown frequency is not above the unconditional drawdown frequency.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nominal retail demand combined with stagnant industrial production and rising CPI signals supply-constrained demand that compresses margins, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month growth is positive, INDPRO 3-month growth is below 0.5%, and CPIAUCSL year-over-year change crosses above 3%
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL latest available value was 237.566 as of t-2mo in the decision snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if the joint supply-constraint trigger has a positive 5-day S&P 500 hit rate above 55%, or if the three-series interaction term fails to outperform all single-proxy models out of sample.
